channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harris is laying out a new plan to empower Black men as she tries to energize them to vote for her
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris is announcing a series of proposals designed to give Black men more economic opportunities as she works to energize a key voting bloc that has Democrats concerned about a lack of enthusiasm.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Most AAPI adults think legal immigrants give the US a major economic boost: AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll
item.description: According to a new poll, Asian American, Native Hawaiian and Pacific Islander adults are more likely than the overall U.S. population to view legal immigration as an asset to the country’s economy and workforce.
item.dc:creator: Terry Tang And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: This could have been a year of a federal court reckoning for Trump. Judges had other ideas
item.description: A year that began with the prospect of a federal court reckoning for Trump will conclude without any chance at trial, leaving voters without an up-or-down jury verdict in the two most consequential cases against the Republican presidential nominee.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Walz defends using the term 'socialism': 'It's what the right uses'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) on Sunday defended his use of the word "socialism" over the summer, saying he believes "capitalism works" but that there are things society must do collectively.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Scully, NewsNation

item.title: Inside the TikTok documents: Stripping teens and boosting ‘attractive’ people
item.description: A trove of secret documents show teens’ increasingly reliant on TikTok and how executives were acutely aware of the potential harm the app can cause young people, but appeared unconcerned.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, Sylvia Goodman, and Dara Kerr, MPR News

item.title: Harris helps pack aid boxes for North Carolina hurricane victims ahead of Sunday campaign push
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris on Saturday helped pack diapers into boxes of personal care products destined for North Carolina hurricane victims, agreeing with one helper who said “it takes a village.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump hears at a Latino campaign event from someone who lived in the US illegally
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Minutes after Donald Trump delivered his standard warnings about drug dealers and criminals illegally crossing the border during a Saturday campaign event, the former president heard from someone who was once in the country illegally but now plans to vote for him.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fight for House raging in districts ringing NYC where Trump struggled but other Republicans thrived
item.description: NELSONVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — Voters in a ring of congressional districts encircling New York City where Republican candidates often do well but Donald Trump struggled in 2020 could decide which party controls the U.S. House for the next two years.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris outpaces Trump in Pennsylvania, but trails in Arizona: Poll
item.description: The New York Times/Philadelphia Inquirer/Siena College survey, released Saturday, shows Harris leading Trump 50 percent to 47 percent among likely voters in the Keystone State.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Hunting for male voters: Walz steps into the field for the pheasant opener
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz had more pheasant hunting companions than usual for this year’s opener near Sleepy Eye, on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hovland and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Donald Trump, Kamala Harris still neck-and-neck in swing states
item.description: Data coming out of critical swing states show that any polling margins remain razor thin and within the margin of error
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Key disaster loan program 'quickly exhausting' funds after hurricanes
item.description: Officials are warning a key federal program that provides disaster loans to businesses and homeowners is running critically low on funds in the aftermath of back-to-back hurricanes, increasing the pressure on Congress to return to Washington.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: No Dem effort to stymie Trump election win certification: Strategists
item.description: A few Democratic lawmakers say they will accept a free and fair Trump election win, but aren't sure he'll be fair or honest.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Comer slams Raskin over his election certification comments: 'Ultimate hypocrite'
item.description: House Oversight Chairman James Comer (R-Ky.) condemned comments from Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-Md.), calling him “the ultimate hypocrite” after the Democrat claimed former President Trump is trying to manipulate the electoral college.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Walz returns to Mankato to reprise role as coach, deliver football pep talk and campaign a little
item.description: The governor stopped through on a campaign visit marking the 25th anniversary of the season the Mankato West football team won the state championship with him as assistant coach. He will attend the Governors Pheasant opener in Sleepy Eye Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Maria Torres-Springer discusses new role as NYC’s first deputy mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams on Tuesday appointed Maria Torres-Springer to be New York City’s new first deputy mayor after Sheena Wright resigned from her position.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson discusses government transparency, public safety, and housing
item.description: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide Friday to discuss issues she believes need to be addressed in the city.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Battle to shore up bases remains as Election Day nears
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and his guest talks about deadly conflicts in the Middle East and how those have factored into domestic politics. Plus, we follow up with two Generation Z voters about how they’re approaching the election.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Abortion has passed inflation as the top election issue for women under 30, survey finds
item.description: About 2 in 5 in the group of young voters said abortion was their top concern in the recent survey, compared with 1 in 5 who ranked it most important in the same survey in the spring.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Senate candidates steer clear of debates
item.description: Senate candidates this cycle are largely steering clear of the debate stage, in some cases avoiding it altogether, marking a major change from past years as campaigns question the debates' importance. For years, it was common for top Senate races to feature multiple debates.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Speculation continues over Cuomo run for NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- There has been growing speculation this week that former Gov. Andrew Cuomo is rapidly approaching a decision about whether or not to run for New York City mayor.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: The disconnect between economic data and voters' concerns
item.description: Are you worried about money? Concerned about the economy? MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests talk about the financial issues affecting how people plan to vote.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: Former Sen. Tim Johnson, the last Democrat to hold statewide office in South Dakota, dies at 77
item.description: Former Sen. Tim Johnson, a centrist who was the last Democrat to hold statewide office in South Dakota and who spent nearly three decades in Congress, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Election drop boxes open in San Diego County
item.description: While mail ballots are expected to arrive this week for registered voters, 150 official ballot drop boxes opened Tuesday in San Diego County for the November Presidential General Election.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: FBI arrests Afghan man who officials say planned Election Day attack in the U.S.
item.description: Nasir Ahmad Tawhedi, 27, of Oklahoma City told investigators after his arrest Monday that he had planned his attack to coincide with Election Day next month and that he and a co-conspirator expected to die as martyrs, according to charging documents.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump secretly sent Putin COVID-19 tests during pandemic shortage, a new book reports
item.description: Vice President Harris is criticizing Trump over the reported shipment, which is detailed in a new book by the journalist Bob Woodward. The president's campaign has denied the reporting.
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: Meet the candidates running for Congress in San Diego County
item.description: As Election Day nears, voters in San Diego County will soon get to choose who will represent San Diego County in the U.S. House of Representatives.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Lombardo among GOP governors criticizing Biden-Harris immigration asylum actions
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo was among 25 Republican governors who signed a letter criticizing the Biden-Harris administration over asylum policies that have brought more than a half-million immigrants into the United States.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harris, Walz appeared on ABC's 'The View' and 'Jimmy Kimmel Live'
item.description: The Democratic presidential ticket is on a media blitz in New York. Vice President Kamala Harris appeared Tuesday on ABC's “The View," and her running mate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz appeared on ABC's “Jimmy Kimmel Live” on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Melania Trump waves off 'hand-swatting incident': A 'mere misunderstanding'
item.description: Melania Trump is waving off speculation that she didn't want to hold her husband's hand, chalking up images of her seemingly "swatting" away former President Donald Trump to a "mere misunderstanding.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump campaign blasts Bob Woodward; denies secret Putin calls
item.description: Donald Trump's campaign is rejecting claims in Bob Woodward's new book 'War' about the former president holding secret calls with Vladimir Putin since leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Your guide to voting in Minnesota: Registration, absentee ballots and general election details
item.description: We are less than a month out from Election Day. Here are answers to some frequently asked questions MPR News readers asked us about the upcoming election.
item.dc:creator: Anika Besst and Nicole Johnson, MPR News

item.title: Michael Naft discusses traffic safety, F1, and animal welfare in the Las Vegas valley
item.description: Michael Naft, incumbent county commissioner for District 8, joined Brian Loftis on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide Monday, discussing his campaign priorities as election day nears.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court won’t hear Pennsylvania legislators' challenge to executive actions expanding voting access
item.description: The Supreme Court decided Monday it won’t take up a case to restore a lawsuit by Pennsylvania Republicans that challenged executive actions expanding voting access on the basis that only state legislatures can regulate federal elections.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Major North Carolina newspaper knocks Trump over Helene response 'falsehoods'
item.description: A leading newspaper in North Carolina is blasting former President Donald Trump over what it calls his "falsehoods" about the government response to Hurricane Helene, which devastated large swaths of the state last week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear arguments in Texas emergency abortion case
item.description: The Supreme Court decided not to hear arguments in a case involving Texas that could have provided an answer about whether a state abortion ban conflicts with a federal emergency care law.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: GOP senator calls Trump special counsel filing 'a temper tantrum from a deranged fanatic'
item.description: Arkansas Sen. Tom Cotton (R) said in a Sunday interview that a recently unsealed filing from special counsel Jack Smith is “a temper tantrum from a deranged fanatic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5
